title: Charmed Life — contents (Fragment 1)
sourceText:
Contents 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Contents

 
 Note: T HERE ARE T HOUSANDS of worlds, all different from ours.

 
 1 C AT C HANT A DMIRED his elder sister Gwendolen. She was a witch.

 
 2 C AT WAS A LARMED by Miss Larkins. She was the daughter of Mr. Larkins at the junk shop.

 
 3 T HE T RAIN J OURNEY lasted about an hour, before the train puffed into Bowbridge. . .

 
 4 T HE S AME S OFTNESS and silence were there when the red-haired Mary woke Cat. . .

 
 5 G WENDOLEN R EFUSED TO tell Cat what she was going to do.

 
 6 G WENDOLEN G AVE V ENT to her fury in her room after dinner.

 
 7 I T R AINED ON Saturday afternoon. Gwendolen shut herself into her room. . .

 
 8 G WENDOLEN W ENT D OWN to the village to get her dragons’ blood on Wednesday afternoon.

 
 9 B Y T HIS T IME , Cat was as bewildered as the strange girl seemed to be. Chant?

 
 10 C AT W ATCHED H IS moment and, when Mr. Saunders called them to lessons. . .

 
 11 B Y THE T IME Janet had laced both her boots, Cat was sure it was lunchtime.

 
 12 C HRESTOMANCI’S S TUDY WAS a large, sun-filled room with books in shelves. . .

 
 13 T HEY WERE U SHERED into a private parlor in the White Hart.

 
 14 C AT S CREAMED . Flames burst out of him all over. He screamed again. . .

 
 15 I T WAS A G LORIOUS start-of-autumn day, with everything green and gold, hot and still.

 
 16 “C AT,” SAID C HRESTOMANCI , from almost behind Cat’s head. “Cat!”

 HarperCollins e-book Exclusive Extras: 
 Who Is Chrestomanci?

 A BOUT THE A UTHOR 
 
 

 
 B OOKS BY D IANA W YNNE J ONES 
 
 

 
 C REDITS 
 
 

 
 A BOUT THE P UBLISHER 
 
 

 
 C OPYRIGHT

words.definition: Confused or puzzled completely.
words.example: By this time, Cat was as bewildered as the strange girl seemed to be.
words.exampleText: By this time, Cat was as bewildered as the strange girl seemed to be.

words.definition: Intense, often violent anger.
words.example: Gwendolen gave vent to her fury in her room after dinner.
words.exampleText: Gwendolen gave vent to her fury in her room after dinner.

words.definition: Delightful; wonderful; deserving admiration.
words.example: It was a glorious start-of-autumn day, with everything green and gold, hot and still.
words.exampleText: It was a glorious start-of-autumn day, with everything green and gold, hot and still.

words.definition: Declined to do something.
words.example: Gwendolen refused to tell Cat what she was going to do.
words.exampleText: Gwendolen refused to tell Cat what she was going to do.

words.definition: Showed or led someone somewhere.
words.example: They were ushered into a private parlor in the White Hart.
words.exampleText: They were ushered into a private parlor in the White Hart.

words.definition: A particular point in time.
words.example: Cat watched his moment and, when Mr. Saunders called them to lessons...
words.exampleText: Cat watched his moment and, when Mr. Saunders called them to lessons...

words.definition: Flat surfaces used to hold things, often books.
words.example: Chrestomanci's study was a large, sun-filled room with books in shelves.
words.exampleText: Chrestomanci's study was a large, sun-filled room with books in shelves.

words.definition: Complete absence of sound.
words.example: The same softness and silence were there when the red-haired Mary woke Cat.
words.exampleText: The same softness and silence were there when the red-haired Mary woke Cat.

words.definition: The act of travelling by train from one place to another.
words.example: The train journey lasted about an hour, before the train puffed into Bowbridge.
words.exampleText: The train journey lasted about an hour, before the train puffed into Bowbridge.

words.definition: To blow out air or smoke.
words.example: Before the train puffed into Bowbridge.
words.exampleText: Before the train puffed into Bowbridge.

words.definition: A red resin often used historically in medicine and magic contexts.
words.example: Gwendolen went down to the village to get her dragons’ blood on Wednesday afternoon.
words.exampleText: Gwendolen went down to the village to get her dragons’ blood on Wednesday afternoon.

words.definition: A title/name referring to a powerful enchanter or magical official in the story.
words.example: Chrestomanci's study was a large, sun-filled room with books in shelves.
words.exampleText: Chrestomanci's study was a large, sun-filled room with books in shelves.

words.definition: A woman thought to have magical powers.
words.example: She was a witch.
words.exampleText: She was a witch.

words.definition: A family name; used here to indicate a person related to the junk shop owner.
words.example: She was the daughter of Mr. Larkins at the junk shop.
words.exampleText: She was the daughter of Mr. Larkins at the junk shop.

words.definition: A shop that sells old or discarded items.
words.example: She was the daughter of Mr. Larkins at the junk shop.
words.exampleText: She was the daughter of Mr. Larkins at the junk shop.

words.definition: To utter a loud, piercing cry.
words.example: Cat screamed. Flames burst out of him all over. He screamed again.
words.exampleText: Cat screamed. Flames burst out of him all over. He screamed again.

words.definition: Hot, glowing gases that rise from a fire.
words.example: Flames burst out of him all over.
words.exampleText: Flames burst out of him all over.

words.definition: To fasten or tighten with a lace or string.
words.example: Janet had laced both her boots.
words.exampleText: Janet had laced both her boots.

words.definition: A sitting room or private room for entertaining guests.
words.example: They were ushered into a private parlor in the White Hart.
words.exampleText: They were ushered into a private parlor in the White Hart.

words.definition: Older or senior in age or rank.
words.example: Cat Chant admired his elder sister Gwendolen.
words.exampleText: Cat Chant admired his elder sister Gwendolen.

words.definition: Complete absence of sound.
words.example: The same softness and silence were there when the red-haired Mary woke Cat.
words.exampleText: The same softness and silence were there when the red-haired Mary woke Cat.

words.definition: A list or collection of items in a book or document.
words.example: Contents
words.exampleText: Contents

words.definition: A brief record or comment to provide information or explanation.
words.example: Note: There are thousands of worlds, all different from ours.
words.exampleText: Note: There are thousands of worlds, all different from ours.

words.definition: Regarded with respect or approval.
words.example: Cat Chant admired his elder sister Gwendolen.
words.exampleText: Cat Chant admired his elder sister Gwendolen.

words.definition: To express or release a strong emotion.
words.example: Gwendolen gave vent to her fury in her room after dinner.
words.exampleText: Gwendolen gave vent to her fury in her room after dinner.
